summary: Promote the MPI-308 caption harness to a context-menu action (gallery card + history item, video variant = current frame) running as a NORMAL QUEUED generation with status-bar progress, caption injected into the prompt box positive field. Trigger moves off the prompt box chip; destination stays the prompt box. FORCES the text-only completion contract MPI-308 bypassed: generationService if(!urls.length) treats zero-media as CANCELLED and discards promptText, and this card cannot bypass it because it needs the queue+progress. Fix = op-level outputKind declaration, NOT a guard clause at the gate. Recon done: context menu items arrays are SEPARATE in MpiGalleryGrid.js ~1107 and MpiHistoryList.js ~229 (shared renderer, add entry TWICE); onSelect receives only key, parent block resolves filePath; _isVideo conditional pattern already exists; captureFrameBlob/captureSnapshot for video frames (must write to a real path, MpiLoadImageFromPath reads OS paths not blobs); injectPrompts for the write. NO work needed: StatusBar (short label only, caption goes to prompt box), progressStages (text workflow emits zero tqdm bars), queue panel (already falls back to a TEXT-labelled empty frame). OUT OF SCOPE: sidecar storage + auto-feeding upscale.

Replaced the caption->upscale gate with the real rationale. The captioner is NOT upscale-only: the primary workflow is describe -> EDIT the text -> generate (reference image -> caption -> adjust toward a target character -> paint face -> LoRA detail -> Head Swap app). The caption is an editable STARTING POINT, which is exactly why it belongs in the prompt box rather than stored and auto-consumed. This value is already demonstrated by the MPI-308 harness and does not depend on the unproven caption->upscale claim, so the card is no longer gated on that comparison. Upscale re-captioning stays a separate future consumer of the same op.

STEP 0 added and card flagged attention:required. The 5.24GB abliterated captioner weight fits NEITHER existing owner: not a MODEL (nobody generates with it; a ModelDef forces dead workflows/qualityTiers/mediaType fields) and not an APP (trigger is a context-menu action, not an App Library tile). USER DECIDED: introduce a third entity, a PLUGIN, with its own registry — { id, title, requiredDeps, operation }, nothing model-shaped. Rejected: ModelDef+isPlugin flag (dead fields, every model consumer must honour it), requiredDeps on the Head Swap app (wrong owner, general capability), reusing stock krea2-qwen3vl-clip (refusals return). MUST SOLVE: (1) install-on-demand gating since no model pulls it; (2) GC — both uninstall guards in routes/downloadManager.js build protected sets from MODELS unioned with _appRequiredDepIds(), so a dep owned by neither is INVISIBLE and any model uninstall deletes it; the plugin registry must be unioned into BOTH; (3) Pod — do not bake 5.24GB into the image for an optional captioner. Precedent: MPI-304 app requiredDeps, generalised one step. Dep entry still filed by KIND in assetDeps.js beside krea2-qwen3vl-clip.

note: todo -> doing. STEP 0 (plugin entity + GC guards + dep entry) built and tested.
All 6 steps shipped across 4 commits; awaiting one real in-app run.
Krea2 consolidated onto the abliterated encoder (stock deleted R2+disk); identity LoRA v1.2. Encoder is now a real shared dep — step 6 shared-dep uninstall still UNVERIFIED.
Step 6 FAILED destructively: plugin uninstall deleted the 5.24GB shared encoder; Krea 2 Turbo NSFW broken. Handoff written; restart test decides stale-cache vs broken guard.

ROOT CAUSE FOUND + FIXED. The handoff's primary hypothesis (stale require cache) was only the PROXIMATE cause; app.log shows TWO deletions (09:46:34 and 16:37:26), both logging 'kept 0 shared'. Server booted 09:39, models.js edited 12:21 (commit 8e852aff 13:02) — so the cache WAS stale at the second delete. But the restart test would have MASKED the real defect: the fullyInstalled gate (the handoff's dismissed secondary hypothesis) is CIRCULAR. A shared COMMON dep is itself an input to deriveInstalledOps' commonComplete, so once the weight went missing every model needing it stopped defending it — the damage was unrecoverable by design, and it cascades across a whole model family (the MPI-258 tier-cycle hole generalised). FIX: protection now keys on DECLARATION + any on-disk footprint, not completeness; installedOps still narrows which ops' deps are protected, and a damaged model falls back to its full universe. Applied to BOTH twins (_localSharedDepsMap + _remoteSharedDepIds) in one pass per the engine-split rule. TEST: tests/shared-dep-uninstall-direction.test.cjs covers the never-run direction (model deps during a PLUGIN uninstall); mutation-verified — case 2 fails against the old gate, passes against the fix. Also removed the dead krea2-qwen3vl-clip dep entry (zero consumers, R2 object deleted so its url 404s) + 2 stale comments. docs/download-manager.md updated with the circularity + the 'dep-graph edits need a SERVER restart' operational trap. NOT YET DONE: user must re-download the 5.24GB weight to unbreak Krea 2 Turbo NSFW, and the fix is logic+unit verified only — a live uninstall on a restarted server is unverified.

FIX v2 — the first fix (dd3f97fa, any-footprint) REINTRODUCED MPI-258 B1. User asked whether MPI-276 was checked: it had not been, and its research/04-bug-history-invariants.md holds invariant 5 (whole-model-installed sharers) for exactly the B1 reason — the pre-258 per-dep rule was circular the OPPOSITE way, stranding ~19GB of LTX tier-family deps as undeletable. Proved empirically: with any-footprint, uninstalling ltx-23 with both transformers absent stranded 11/11 shared deps. REAL RULE: install evidence must come from a model's EXCLUSIVE deps (files no other model declares) — exclusive deps can never be someone else's footprint (kills B1) and can never be the shared file under judgement (kills 310). CRITICAL SCOPING TRAP found live: exclusivity computed against the guard's `others` list is WRONG (it omits the uninstall target, making its shared deps look exclusive to the sibling — B1 again); _multiModelDepIds() computes it over the whole registry. Both twins updated. Test now pins BOTH circularities (mutation-verified both directions: old gate fails case 2, others-scoped exclusivity fails case 4). Plus the UX gap user flagged: a plugin uninstall that frees nothing now toasts 'Nothing was freed — still needed by <models>' (keptShared[].sharedWith surfaced); plugin row stays Installed because pluginAvailability reads disk truth. docs/download-manager.md rewritten with the two-circularities table + pointer to the MPI-276 invariants dossier.

Toast/notification pass (f2ace407, 0f9d8910). User challenged my claim that there was 'no install-completion toast by design' — I had grepped only 2 files and was WRONG. A Downloads desktop-notification toggle exists (MpiSettings, notificationPrefs.downloads) and notificationService DOES notify on download:complete. Two real defects found: (1) DUPLICATE TOAST — MpiModelManager already had a full uninstall toast ladder incl. a 'Nothing to remove' branch, so the toast I added in downloadService (48b76ace) was a second surface; reverted, improved the existing one instead to NAME the holders from keptShared[].sharedWith. (2) UNFOCUSED COMPLETION LOST — the download:complete handler returned after sending the OS notification, so nothing surfaced in-app; a missed OS notification (focus assist/fullscreen/dismissed) left zero trace after a multi-GB install, exactly the walk-away flow the user described. Fixed: unfocused completions queue a toast flushed on the next window `focus` (not visibilitychange — an Electron window can be visible but unfocused). User chose this option over both-immediately / persistent activity list. (3) Same plugin-name bug in BOTH surfaces: modelId is `plugin:<id>`, absent from MODELS, so the raw key leaked into toast + notification text; both now resolve PLUGINS titles. User's design point recorded: install completion deserves notification MORE than uninstall (async + walk-away vs synchronous button press). STILL UNVERIFIED: the live plugin-uninstall test.

doing -> todo, maturity validating. Shared-dep destructive bug CLOSED and user-verified end-to-end: guard kept the encoder ('removed 0, kept 1 shared' x2 in app.log 17:39), weight byte-exact on disk (5,242,481,504), Krea2 raw installed WITHOUT re-fetching the 5.24GB encoder, and Krea2 raw GENERATED successfully — proving the twice-deleted-and-refetched weight is sound, not merely the right size. Toast validated at BOTH holder counts live: 1 holder ('Krea 2 Turbo NSFW also uses them') and 2 holders ('Krea 2 Turbo NSFW, Krea 2 NSFW'). DEFERRED BY USER: RunPod/remote validation. The remote twin _remoteSharedDepIds got the identical exclusive-evidence fix in the same pass (engine-split rule) but has NEVER RUN — logic-verified only. Also still open on this card: plugin-row progress bar, Heretic LoRA, video 'Describe current frame' (MPI-287), remote describer.

summary: Moved MPI-310 from To do to Doing.

DESTRUCTIVE BUG, USER DATA LOST. Plugin uninstall deleted a 5.24GB weight that four Krea2 cards depend on; Krea 2 Turbo NSFW is broken (100% -> 78%) and the encoder must be re-downloaded before Krea2 generates at all. READ handoff-shared-dep-uninstall-bug.md AND docs/download-manager.md before touching routes/downloadManager.js — partial card bars are COMPLETENESS, not download progress, and misreading them sends you down the wrong root cause. First action is the restart test that decides between stale-require-cache and a genuinely broken guard. Fix both engine twins in one pass.
